text:
In a small town called Meadowbrook, three childhood friends—Tom, Jack, and Sara—visited their favorite tree, aptly named Big Oak, every autumn. The tree stood like an ancient sentinel overlooking Main Street, its branches heavy with the amber glow of falling leaves. It was Tom who reminded them annually of their pact.

"My grandma says we should pick three seeds and plant them in different parts of the county," Tom said one crisp autumn afternoon.

Jack gave a grin. "You mean like the Three Little Pigs?"

"Exactly," Tom nodded with a smirk. "And since we've never done anything like this before, it'll be our own little adventure."

Sara chimed in enthusiastically. "Let's do it! We'll each pick a seed and plan where to plant it. It could be anywhere—our garden, a park—it's up to us!"
